name: Natural Pivot Turn
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Cross Chasse, Fish Tail, Forward Lock, Four Quick Run, Quarter Turn to Left, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn, Progressive Chasse, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6, Zig-Zag Back Lock Running Finish
following_figure_names: Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Rumba Cross
notes:
Difference between a Pivot Turn and a Spin Turn is that there is no Rise in Pivot Turn. There is Rise (and brush) in Spin Turn.
At corner: turn 3/8 to face DW on new LOD.
Turn amount is up to 1/2 to face BLOD, followed by any Natural figure.

steps.action: RF forward, FDW, HT, start to turn R, sway S, CBM, start to rise e/o 1
LF side, BDC, T, 1/4 to R between 1-2, sway R, Up
RF closes to LF, BLOD, TH, 1/8 to R between 2-3, sway R, Up, lower e/o 3
LF back (RF held in CBMP), Point BDW, THT, R up to 1/2 turn, sway S, CBM
LF back, BDW, TH, starts to turn R, sway S, CBM, start to rise e/o 1, NFR
comments: Curve to left, give left side forward to Man, keep head very left

steps.action: RF side, PLOD, T, 3/8 between 1-2, body turns less, sway L, Up
comments: RF moving direction is BDW, point LOD

steps.action: LF close to RF, FLOD, TH, body completes turn, sway L, Up, lower e/o 3
comments: Not as much rise, knee stay flexed, drop faster

steps.action: RF forward (pivot), FLOD, HTH, heel kept in contact with floor, up to 1/2 to R, sway S, CBM
comments: Heel is kept in contact with floor
